spec_match.rationale: The page uses the current CSS Values 5 comma-separated progress(value, start, end) grammar and wraps progress() in clamp(0, ..., 1) where bounded threshold UI is required.

interactive.rationale: Temperature, minimum, and maximum sliders update the gauge position, threshold readouts, band meters, and marker state live.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature page and concept copy now frame progress() as an extrapolating math primitive that can be explicitly clamped for threshold UI.

edge_cases.rationale: The demo covers below-range, in-range, above-range, inverted cold thresholds, adjustable endpoints, and a JavaScript fallback when native progress() support is missing.

references.rationale: The page links to the ChromeStatus entry and the CSS Values 5 progress() section.

design_system.rationale: The page uses shared CSS variables, no raw color literals, no style attributes, no inline event handlers, and explicit foreground/background colors for status badges.

summary: Resolved the stale syntax critique by preserving the spec-current comma-separated progress() grammar. The demo also now states that progress() can extrapolate and uses clamp(0, progress(...), 1) anywhere the UI needs a bounded 0..1 threshold.
